evidence.scientific_clarity: Force-directed layout clearly separates four communities by color; node labels (IDs) are readable at size 6 but add clutter without scientific value.
evidence.typography: Script never sets pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype='none'; pdffonts confirms Type 3 (bitmap) fonts (DejaVuSans-Bold, DejaVuSans) — not editable vector text. Default DejaVu Sans used throughout.
evidence.palette: Four-color palette (#E63946 red, #457B9D blue, #2A9D8F teal, #E9C46A yellow) is reasonably colorblind-safe for deuteranopia/protanopia; red is used categorically (not directionally), which is acceptable here.
evidence.layout_density: 6×6 inch single panel is well-used; tight_layout applied; edge alpha=0.25 reduces hairball; no overlapping labels observed at this node count.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and mentions the algorithm; legend is placed lower-right and is clear; no axis labels needed (axis off); caption lacks a quantitative finding (e.g., modularity score or edge count).
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF is 18 KB and contains real vector paths for nodes/edges, but fonts are Type 3 (not Type 42) — text is not selectable/editable in Illustrator or Inkscape.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all exist and are non-trivial (PDF 18 KB, PNG 97 KB, SVG 49 KB).
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names the algorithm (Fruchterman-Reingold), mentions edge transparency, and references data.json, but provides no numerical claim (e.g., number of edges, modularity, or community sizes).

overall_strengths: All required output files are present and non-trivial; SVG is also produced as a bonus.
Palette and layout choices (edge alpha, node size, spring layout tuning) successfully avoid the hairball problem for a 30-node graph.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype is never set to 42 (nor svg.fonttype to 'none'), so all text in the PDF is Type 3 bitmap — not editable vector text, failing a core typography requirement.
Caption contains no quantitative finding (no modularity score, edge count, or community size), reducing its scientific utility for a reviewer.
